element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-like environment establishing core movement and shooting
Environmental navigation mechanic carried from previous X games
Weapon selection strategy; each boss weak to specific weapon type
Optional upgrades found in levels; cumulative power progression
Single-chip slot system; player choice-driven customization
Life extension and energy storage mechanics from prior games
Pilotable vehicles with distinct gameplay feel; stage-specific placement
Alternative character with restrictions: no armor/power-ups, no boss fights (except one secret)
Strategic order completion based on weapon weakness chain
Late-game mandatory progression; unlocked after initial eight-stage completion

typical_relief_triggers: Vehicle (Ride Armor) sections providing temporary invulnerability
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy robot encounters requiring special weapon strategy
Zero depletion (permanent loss for current playthrough)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Moderate to fast; alternates between exploration/discovery segments and combat encounters; non-linear stage select maintains player agency over pacing

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Capsule placement (armor parts, chips, Heart Tanks, Sub-Tanks) hidden within levels signals secrets; Ride Armor summon items discoverable
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Vertical exploration and hidden capsules; wall-cling mechanics enable access to off-path areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction through intro stage; first selected Maverick stage establishes baseline challenge based on player's weapon choice
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive difficulty increases as stages are completed; player gains weapon variety and upgrades; non-linear progression allows difficulty modulation through stage order selection
difficulty_curve.late_game: Maverick boss encounters scale in complexity; Dr. Doppler stages present mandatory sequential challenge with no weapon choice flexibility
notable_spikes: First Maverick boss encounter (difficulty depends on player-selected stage)
Transition to Dr. Doppler stages (four mandatory sequential stages with limited weapon variety)

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight independently selectable stages with thematic environmental obstacles (e.g., Toxic Seahorse's toxic liquid, Blast Hornet's aerial sections). Boss weak to specific special weapon. Player chooses order to optimize weapon-weakness chain.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Toxic Seahorse stage; Blast Hornet stage (featured in prototype docs)

notable_level_patterns.description: Introductory level establishing core mechanics with Zero as helper character. Serves tutorial function before stage select.
Pilotable vehicle segments integrated into specific stages; provides temporary power and altered movement mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Various stages (specific locations not detailed in sources)

notable_level_patterns.description: Four mandatory sequential stages unlocked after defeating all eight Mavericks. Linear progression through final bosses leading to Sigma confrontation. No weapon choice optimization possible.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Doppler Stage 1-4 (referenced as unlockable after initial eight stages)

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Mega Man X3 (SNES) article
wikipedia - Mega Man X3 Wikipedia article
